00001| """
00002| Multi-Period Smart Savings Test Data Generator
00003| Generates data for different time periods to test filtering
00004| """
00005| 
00006| import requests
00007| import random
00008| import time
00009| from datetime import datetime, timedelta
00010| 
00011| API_URL = "http://localhost:5000"
00012| COST_PER_KWH = 0.12
00013| 
00014| # Device configurations
00015| DEVICES_CONFIG = {
00016|     "TV": {"power_on": 120, "power_standby": 3, "voltage": 230},
00017|     "Router": {"power_on": 15, "power_standby": 12, "voltage": 230},
00018|     "Charger": {"power_on": 25, "power_standby": 0.5, "voltage": 230},
00019|     "Refrigerator": {"power_on": 200, "power_standby": 50, "voltage": 230}
00020| }
00021| 
00022| def generate_device_data(device_name, config, hours_ago=0, period='month'):
00023|     """Generate device data for specific time period"""
00024|     hour_of_day = (datetime.now().hour - hours_ago) % 24
00025|     
00026|     # Determine if data should be included based on period
00027|     days_ago = hours_ago // 24
00028|     
00029|     if period == 'today' and days_ago >= 1:
00030|         return None
00031|     elif period == 'month' and days_ago >= 30:
00032|         return None
00033|     elif period == 'year' and days_ago >= 365:
00034|         return None
00035|     
00036|     # Simulate different scenarios
00037|     is_auto_controlled = random.random() < 0.3
00038|     is_manual_override = random.random() < 0.1
00039|     
00040|     # Determine power state based on time and automation
00041|     if device_name == "Router":
00042|         power_watts = config["power_on"]
00043|         mode = "ON"
00044|     elif device_name == "Refrigerator":
00045|         if random.random() < 0.7:
00046|             power_watts = config["power_on"] if not is_auto_controlled else config["power_standby"]
00047|             mode = "ON"
00048|         else:
00049|             power_watts = config["power_standby"]
00050|             mode = "Standby"
00051|     elif device_name == "TV":
00052|         if 18 <= hour_of_day <= 23 and days_ago == 0:  # Evening today
00053|             if is_auto_controlled and random.random() < 0.4:
00054|                 power_watts = config["power_standby"]
00055|                 mode = "Standby"
00056|                 is_auto_controlled = True
00057|             else:
00058|                 power_watts = config["power_on"]
00059|                 mode = "ON"
00060|         else:
00061|             power_watts = config["power_standby"]
00062|             mode = "Standby"
00063|     else:  # Charger
00064|         if 7 <= hour_of_day <= 9 or 19 <= hour_of_day <= 23:  # Charging times
00065|             if is_auto_controlled and random.random() < 0.6:
00066|                 power_watts = config["power_standby"]
00067|                 mode = "Standby"
00068|                 is_auto_controlled = True
00069|             else:
00070|                 power_watts = config["power_on"]
00071|                 mode = "ON"
00072|         else:
00073|             power_watts = config["power_standby"]
00074|             mode = "Standby"
00075|     
00076|     return {
00077|         "device_name": device_name,
00078|         "mode": mode,
00079|         "voltage": config["voltage"] + random.uniform(-5, 5),
00080|         "power_watts": power_watts + random.uniform(-2, 2),
00081|         "timestamp": (datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=hours_ago)).isoformat(),
00082|         "auto_controlled": is_auto_controlled,
00083|         "manual_override": is_manual_override
00084|     }
00085| 
00086| def generate_period_data(period, hours_to_generate):
00087|     """Generate data for specific period"""
00088|     print(f"🔄 Generating {hours_to_generate} hours of data for {period.upper()}...")
00089|     
00090|     for hours_ago in range(hours_to_generate, 0, -1):
00091|         for device_name, config in DEVICES_CONFIG.items():
00092|             data = generate_device_data(device_name, config, hours_ago, period)
00093|             
00094|             if data:  # Only add if data should be included for this period
00095|                 response = requests.post(f"{API_URL}/api/electricity/add", json=data)
00096|                 if response.status_code == 201:
00097|                     auto = "🤖" if data["auto_controlled"] else "👤"
00098|                     mode = data["mode"]
00099|                     power = data["power_watts"]
00100|                     print(f"  {auto} {device_name} ({hours_ago}h ago): {power:.1f}W - {mode}")
00101|                 else:
00102|                     print(f"  ❌ Failed to add data for {device_name}")
00103|         
00104|         time.sleep(0.05)  # Small delay to avoid overwhelming API
00105|     
00106|     print(f"✅ {period.upper()} data generation complete!")
00107| 
00108| def add_device_events(period):
00109|     """Add device events for specific period"""
00110|     print(f"📊 Adding {period} device events...")
00111|     
00112|     for device_name, config in DEVICES_CONFIG.items():
00113|         waste_power = config["power_on"] - config["power_standby"]
00114|         
00115|         # Adjust waste/saved hours based on period
00116|         if period == 'today':
00117|             waste_hours = random.uniform(2, 4)
00118|             saved_hours = random.uniform(1, 2)
00119|         elif period == 'month':
00120|             waste_hours = random.uniform(60, 120)
00121|             saved_hours = random.uniform(30, 60)
00122|         elif period == 'year':
00123|             waste_hours = random.uniform(730, 1460)
00124|             saved_hours = random.uniform(365, 730)
00125|         else:
00126|             waste_hours = 60
00127|             saved_hours = 30
00128|         
00129|         # Add waste event
00130|         waste_event = {
00131|             "device_name": device_name,
00132|             "event_type": "waste",
00133|             "power_watts": waste_power,
00134|             "duration_minutes": int(waste_hours * 60),
00135|             "auto_saved": False,
00136|             "timestamp": (datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=12)).isoformat()
00137|         }
00138|         
00139|         response = requests.post(f"{API_URL}/api/device-event", json=waste_event)
00140|         if response.status_code == 201:
00141|             result = response.json()
00142|             print(f"  ❌ {device_name}: ${result['cost_wasted']:.4f} wasted")
00143|         
00144|         # Add saved event (if significant)
00145|         if saved_hours > 1:
00146|             saved_event = {
00147|                 "device_name": device_name,
00148|                 "event_type": "saved",
00149|                 "power_watts": waste_power,
00150|                 "duration_minutes": int(saved_hours * 60),
00151|                 "auto_saved": True,
00152|                 "timestamp": (datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=6)).isoformat()
00153|             }
00154|             
00155|             response = requests.post(f"{API_URL}/api/device-event", json=saved_event)
00156|             if response.status_code == 201:
00157|                 result = response.json()
00158|                 print(f"  ✅ {device_name}: ${result['cost_wasted']:.4f} saved")
00159| 
00160| def test_period_filtering():
00161|     """Test that period filtering works correctly"""
00162|     print("\n🔍 Testing Period Filtering...")
00163|     
00164|     periods = ['today', 'month', 'year']
00165|     for period in periods:
00166|         try:
00167|             response = requests.get(f"{API_URL}/api/savings?period={period}")
00168|             if response.status_code == 200:
00169|                 result = response.json()
00170|                 if result["status"] == "success":
00171|                     data = result["data"]
00172|                     print(f"  ✅ {period.upper()}: Waste=${data['total_waste_cost']:.2f}, Saved=${data['total_saved_cost']:.2f}")
00173|                 else:
00174|                     print(f"  ❌ {period.upper()}: API error")
00175|             else:
00176|                 print(f"  ❌ {period.upper()}: HTTP {response.status_code}")
00177|         except Exception as e:
00178|             print(f"  ❌ {period.upper()}: {e}")
00179| 
00180| def main():
00181|     print("🚀 Multi-Period Smart Savings Test Data Generator")
00182|     print("=" * 60)
00183|     
00184|     # Check if API is running
00185|     try:
00186|         response = requests.get(f"{API_URL}/")
00187|         if response.status_code != 200:
00188|             print("❌ API is not running. Please start the backend first.")
00189|             return
00190|     except:
00191|         print("❌ Cannot connect to API. Please start the backend first.")
00192|         return
00193|     
00194|     # Generate data for all periods
00195|     periods_to_generate = {
00196|         'today': 24,
00197|         'month': 24 * 7,  # 1 week of data
00198|         'year': 24 * 2     # 2 days of data
00199|     }
00200|     
00201|     for period, hours in periods_to_generate.items():
00202|         generate_period_data(period, hours)
00203|         add_device_events(period)
00204|         print()
00205|     
00206|     # Test filtering
00207|     test_period_filtering()
00208|     
00209|     print(f"\n✅ Multi-period data generation complete!")
00210|     print(f"🌐 Open http://localhost:8000 to test the time filter tabs")
00211|     print(f"\n📊 Available periods:")
00212|     for period in ['today', 'month', 'year']:
00213|         print(f"  - {period.upper()}: http://localhost:5000/api/savings?period={period}")
00214| 
00215| if __name__ == "__main__":
00216|     main()

metadata.preview:
"""
Multi-Period Smart Savings Test Data Generator
Generates data for different time periods to test filtering
"""

import requests
import random
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

API_URL = "http://localhost:5000"
COST_PER_KWH = 0.12

# Device configurations
DEVICES_CONFIG = {
    "TV": {"power_on": 120, "power_standby": 3, "voltage": 230},
    "Router": {"power_on": 15, "power_standby": 12, "voltage": 230},
    "Charger": {"power_on": 25, "power_standby": 0.5, "voltage": 230},
    "Refrigerator": {"power_on": 200, "power_standby": 50, "voltage": 230}
}
